skill.name: bash-scripting
skill.description: Bash scripting workflow for creating production-ready shell scripts with defensive patterns, error handling, and testing.
skill.summary: Multi-phase workflow for building robust bash scripts with error handling, logging, and automated testing using Bats and ShellCheck.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 42 patterns but all are false positives. The skill is workflow documentation referencing other skills (@bash-pro, @bash-defensive-patterns) and contains a safe bash template using standard command substitution. No executable security risks detected.

content.user_title: Create production-ready bash scripts with error handling
content.value_statement: Building reliable shell scripts requires defensive patterns and thorough testing. This workflow guides you through creating robust bash scripts with proper error handling, logging, and automated test coverage.
actual_capabilities: Guide through 7-phase bash script development workflow
Implement defensive patterns with strict mode and trap handlers
Create structured logging with timestamps and log levels
Set up Bats testing framework for script validation
Configure ShellCheck for linting and best practices
Generate production-ready script templates with error handling
limitations: Requires other skills (@bash-pro, @bash-defensive-patterns, etc.) to be available
Does not execute scripts - provides guidance and templates only
Testing requires Bats framework installation on target system

use_cases.title: System Administration Automation
use_cases.description: Create reliable scripts for server maintenance, backup operations, and system monitoring with proper error handling and logging.

use_cases.title: CI/CD Pipeline Development
use_cases.description: Build deployment and build scripts with comprehensive testing, rollback capability, and detailed logging for troubleshooting.

use_cases.title: Infrastructure Tooling
use_cases.description: Develop command-line tools for infrastructure management with professional argument parsing, usage documentation, and edge case handling.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Script Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a bash script template with strict mode (set -euo pipefail), usage function, argument parsing, and basic logging. Include cleanup trap handler.

prompt_templates.title: Error Handling Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Add comprehensive error handling to my bash script including trap handlers for EXIT/INT/TERM, retry logic for failed commands, and meaningful exit codes.

prompt_templates.title: Testing Setup with Bats
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate Bats test cases for my bash script covering normal execution, error conditions, edge cases, and argument validation. Include mock functions for external commands.
prompt_templates.scenario: Writing automated tests for script validation

prompt_templates.title: Full Production Script
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through all 7 phases to create a production-ready deployment script with logging, error handling, rollback capability, Bats tests, and ShellCheck configuration.

output_examples.input: Create a backup script with error handling
output: Script template with strict mode enabled
Trap handlers for cleanup on failure
Main backup logic with error checking
Bats test file with 5+ test cases

output_examples.input: Add logging to my existing script
output: Debug mode toggle via VERBOSE flag

best_practices: Always use strict mode (set -euo pipefail) to catch errors early
Quote all variable expansions to handle spaces and special characters
Use ShellCheck before deployment to catch common mistakes
anti_patterns: Using backticks instead of $(...) for command substitution
Ignoring return codes from critical commands
Hardcoding paths instead of using variables or arguments

faq.question: What other skills do I need to use this workflow?
faq.answer: This workflow references @bash-pro, @bash-defensive-patterns, @bash-linux, @bats-testing-patterns, and @shellcheck-configuration. These provide specialized patterns for each phase.

faq.question: Is this workflow compatible with sh or dash?
faq.answer: The workflow targets bash specifically. Some patterns like arrays and process substitution require bash. For POSIX sh compatibility, additional modifications would be needed.

faq.question: How do I run the Bats tests this workflow generates?
faq.answer: Install Bats (brew install bats or apt install bats), then run 'bats tests/' in your script directory. The workflow generates a tests/ folder with .bats test files.

faq.question: What is ShellCheck and why is it recommended?
faq.answer: ShellCheck is a static analysis tool for shell scripts that catches common mistakes, syntax errors, and portability issues. Run it with 'shellcheck script.sh' before deployment.

faq.question: Can I use this workflow for one-off scripts?
faq.answer: Yes, but you can skip phases based on script criticality. For quick scripts, focus on Phase 2 (structure) and Phase 4 (error handling). Full workflow is for production scripts.

faq.question: How do I handle secrets in bash scripts?
faq.answer: Never hardcode secrets. Use environment variables, read from protected files, or use secret management tools. The workflow's error handling should mask sensitive values in logs.
